title: Matelea lanata
intro: Matelea lanata, also known as woolly milkvine or woolly dogbane, is a perennial vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of cardiovascular health, and potential antiviral and antibacterial activities. The bioactive constituents of Matelea lanata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Matelea lanata is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Matelea lanata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The genus Matelea is part of the subfamily Rauvoloideae. Matelea lanata is a native species of the Americas, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Within the Apocynaceae family, Matelea lanata is further grouped based on its morphological and genetic features. Matelea lanata's classification is subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical nomenclature.
